2606.09655.title: Beyond Accuracy: Community Perspectives on Machine Translation
2606.09655.paper_summary: Despite remarkable progress in machine translation (MT), non-AI communities have raised growing concerns about MT systems, suggesting a noticeable gap between technical advancement and the needs of real-world users. For instance, while NLP researchers focus on benchmark performance, end users care about ethical concerns, trust, reliability, costs, and more. We argue that listening to various user communities is essential so that research efforts would be directed towards the problems that the communities care about. To this end, we present a large-scale analysis, for the first time, that investigates what four stakeholder communities (AI developers, professional translators, language learners, and language service providers) post about MT technology on social media. To do so, we construct a dataset of 79,286 posts and comments from Reddit, Facebook, Bluesky, and Mastodon from 2019 to 2025, and analyse where these communities disagree, and how and why. Overall, we find that communities often disagree, and even show strong conflicts due to polarised sentiments on topics such as translation quality, efficiency, and reliability. This is because these communities approach these topics differently: the AI community frames them as technical and computational problems, while non-AI (user) communities care more about quality nuances, time savings, user trust, and broader social issues.
2606.09655.paper_summary_zh: 儘管機器翻譯（MT）取得了顯著進展，非人工智慧社群對MT系統的擔憂日益增加，顯示出技術進步與現實用戶需求之間的明顯差距。<lb/>例如，雖然自然語言處理研究者專注於基準性能，但最終用戶更關心倫理問題、信任、可靠性、成本等。<lb/>我們認為，傾聽各種用戶社群的聲音至關重要，這樣研究工作才能朝著社群關心的問題進行。<lb/>為此，我們首次呈現一項大規模分析，調查四個利益相關者社群（AI開發者、專業翻譯者、語言學習者和語言服務提供者）在社交媒體上對MT技術的發言。<lb/>為了實現這一目標，我們構建了一個包含79,286條來自Reddit、Facebook、Bluesky和Mastodon的帖子和評論的數據集，涵蓋2019年至2025年，並分析這些社群在哪些方面存在分歧，以及如何和為什麼產生這些分歧。<lb/>總體而言，我們發現社群之間經常存在分歧，甚至因對翻譯質量、效率和可靠性等話題的極端情緒而出現強烈衝突。<lb/>這是因為這些社群對這些話題的看法不同：AI社群將其視為技術和計算問題，而非AI（用戶）社群則更關心質量細微差別、時間節省、用戶信任和更廣泛的社會問題。
2606.09655.author: Yujun Wang et.al.
2606.09655.authors: Yujun Wang, Ehud Reiter, Shimei Pan, Steffen Eger, Wei Zhao
